data.title: Synchrony credit card net charge-offs improve in July, delinquencies stable
data.description:
Synchrony Financial (NYSE:SYF) credit card delinquencies stayed stable in July, while net charge-offs dipped, according to a company filing.

The delinquency rate for accounts 30-days or more past due was 4.2%, unchanged from the prior two months, down from 4.6% a year ago, and roughly even from the pre-pandemic level of 4.1% in July 2019. 

The adjusted net charge-off rate of 5.1% dropped from 5.7% in June and 6.3% in July 2024. The metric also compares favorably with the 5.4% level in 2019.

Lending activity edged up from June, with the period-end receivables sitting at $100.3B, up 0.5% M/M and down 2.3% from July 2024.

data.title: T1 Energy surges on deal with Corning for 'Made in America' solar panels
data.description:
T1 Energy (NYSE:TE) +12.7% pre-market Friday after unveiling an agreement with Corning (NYSE:GLW) that the companies said will establish a fully domestic solar supply chain, connecting polysilicon, wafers, cells, and panels manufactured in the U.S.

Under the deal terms, T1 (NYSE:TE) will source hyper-pure polysilicon and solar wafers produced by Corning (NYSE:GLW) from its Michigan factory starting in H2 2026, which will be delivered to T1’s Austin, Texas, solar cell facility, which is currently under development, and the cells will then be used to manufacture solar modules at T1's operational Dallas site.

Corning (NYSE:GLW) reached a similar deal with solar manufacturers Suniva and Heliene earlier this year.

T1 (NYSE:TE) said the deal will help satisfy rising U.S. demand for panels produced with American-made components, particularly in light of President Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill Act, which restricts solar companies from claiming federal clean energy tax credits if projects contain meaningful amounts of equipment produced by foreign entities.

"This landmark supply chain agreement with Corning will help invigorate America with scalable, reliable, low-cost energy," T1 (TE) CEO Daniel Barcelo said. "This is American companies building in America and protecting American energy security."

data.title: LanzaTech to execute 1-for-100 reverse stock split
data.description:
• LanzaTech Global (NASDAQ:LNZA) will carry out a 1-for-100 reverse stock split on August 18, 2025, along with changes to par value and authorized shares.
  • Immediately prior to effecting the reverse stock split, LanzaTech will decrease the par value of the common stock from $0.0001 to $0.0000001 per share and increase the number of authorized shares from 600M to 2.58B.
  • At the effective time of the split, the number of authorized shares will be proportionately decreased to 25.8M.
  • The move aims to meet Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement.
  • LNZA -12.11% premarket.

data.title: Precigen surges on FDA approval for gene therapy
data.description:
Shares of Precigen (NASDAQ:PGEN) added ~34% in the premarket on Friday after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved its gene therapy candidate Papzimeos (zopapogene imadenovec-drba) for a tumor condition called recurrent respiratory papillomatosis (RRP).

According to the FDA, the adenoviral vector-based immunotherapy will be available for adults with RRP, a rare chronic disease characterized by benign tumors in the respiratory tract, often in the larynx.

Early this year, the agency accepted Precigen’s (NASDAQ:PGEN) biologics license application for the treatment, granting priority review with a target action date of Aug. 25.

The BLA was supported by data from a pivotal Phase 1/2 clinical study, which achieved its primary objectives, as more than 50% of patients reported complete remissions with no need for RRP surgeries within 12 months after receiving Papzimeos.

“Randomized trials are not always needed to approve medical products, and this approval is proof of that philosophy,” said Vinay Prasad, who recently returned to the FDA to lead the agency’s Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER).

“The FDA will always demand the correct clinical study for the specific medical product and disease.”

data.title: GIBO to execute 200-for-1 share consolidation
data.description:
• GIBO Holdings (NASDAQ:GIBO) will implement a 200-for-1 share consolidation of its issued and unissued Class A ordinary shares and Class B ordinary shares, effective August 20, 2025.
  • The move aims to boost the market price per share of the company's Class A ordinary shares.
  • GIBO -13.7% premarket.
